_comment: Solver and energy-grid settings for Famiano (2002) reproduction.
Energy grid:
Famiano used EVENLY SPACED bins of 5 MeV for a 400 MeV 4He jet.
For a 400 MeV jet, 80 bins of 5 MeV covering 0–400 MeV is appropriate.
Set type='linear', e_min=2.5, e_max=402.5, n_bins=80 for this.
Timestep:
gamma = 0.01 per Famiano eq. (4) and text (Section 3).
Initial dt: use estimate_initial_dt() from the initial dY/dt.
Integration method:
Famiano used the IMPLICIT Euler method (eq. 2), not explicit Euler.
The jacobian.py module supports this; run_famiano.py currently uses
EXPLICIT Euler (first-order) which is adequate for the non-thermal
Model B (no thermonuclear reactions). For Model A (hot dense clump),
switch to implicit integration using jacobian.py.
Run time:
Famiano runs until the cloud mass increases by a large factor.
For Model B: mass_rate = 1e-6 Msun/yr on a 1e6 Msun cloud → slow.
The time axis in Famiano's figures is fractional mass change DM/M0.
A sensible t_end for initial testing is 1e6 yr = 3.15e13 s.

time._comment: tmax_s = 1e5 s (~1 day); Famiano (2002) bottom x-axis is in seconds (~10^4 s); dt_s is initial guess only

_comment: Famiano: 'energy bin width was taken to be ~5 MeV for a 400 MeV 4He jet'.
Linear grid, 80 bins × 5 MeV = 400 MeV range.
e_min = 2.5 MeV (half-bin below 5), e_max = 402.5 MeV.
Injection bin is bin #79 (0-indexed) at E ~ 397.5–402.5 MeV.

solver._comment: gamma = 0.01 per Famiano eq. (4) Section 3; dt_min/max scaled for seconds-timescale run
